lynx.response:
Based on the project's source code analysis:

- **Language**: TypeScript — the project uses `.ts` and `.tsx` files extensively, with TypeScript (v5) as a dev dependency and `tsx` for running scripts.
- **Framework**: **Next.js** (v15.5.7) with **React** (v19.1.0) — confirmed by the `next` dependency, `next build`/`next start` scripts, `eslint-config-next`, and the `src/app` (App Router) directory structure for pages and API routes.
- **Database**: **PostgreSQL** — confirmed by the Prisma schema definition (`datasource db { provider = "postgresql" }`) with Prisma as the ORM.

```json
{
  "language": "TypeScript",
  "framework": "Next.js (with React)",
  "database": "PostgreSQL"
}
```

lynx.response:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Here is the technical orientation for the WORKMAT project:

```json
{
  "primary_language": "TypeScript / React (Next.js App Router)",
  "entry_points": [
    "src/app/api/admin/analytics/overview/route.ts (GET - analytics dashboard)",
    "src/app/api/chat/route.ts (Chat session management)",
    "src/app/api/admin/mailing/route.ts (Mailing campaign admin)",
    "src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts (Authentication)",
    "src/app/api/deploy/route.ts (Deployment pipeline)",
    "src/app/[slug]/page.tsx (Public landing pages)",
    "src/app/components/SolutionsLanding.tsx (Main WORKMAT landing component)",
    "src/lib/maintenance/index.ts (Background maintenance jobs)",
    "src/components/call/CallWidget.tsx (Client call widget)",
    "src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx (Client chat widget)"
  ],
  "hotspots": [
    "src/app/api/admin/analytics/overview/route.ts (map fn - 309 fan-in, analytics pipeline with remote/prod data fetching)",
    "src/lib/chatActionAuth.ts (Token auth for chat actions - 166 fan-in, high complexity at 23)",
    "src/lib/mailing/service.ts (600+ line mailing service with campaign, tracking, worker logic)",
    "src/components/ContactForm.tsx (Contact form with 249+ fan-in, assistant bridge integration)",
    "src/app/admin/sections/AnalyticsSection.tsx (Admin analytics dashboard, complexity 51)",
    "src/app/api/vapi_chat/route.ts (Vapi voice chat integration, 194 fan-in)",
    "src/lib/backups.ts (Backup serialization utilities, high coupling)",
    "src/app/admin/mailing/page.tsx (Mailing admin page, high error/filter coupling)",
    "src/components/call/CallWidget.tsx (Call widget component, highly coupled)",
    "src/lib/companyProfile.ts (Company profile reader, 110+ reads)"
  ],
  "relevant_tests": [
    "src/components/ContactForm.tsx (inline test function - integration/animation typing test)",
    "src/lib/mailing/service.ts (testSendTemplate fn - sends preview emails)",
    "src/lib/auth/totp.ts (generateTotpTokenForTesting - TOTP token generation test helper)",
    "src/lib/mailing/service.ts (getLatestTemplateTestSend - test send audit trail)"
  ],
  "summary": "WORKMAT is a Next.js (App Router) project for an external agent communication system powering the WORKMAT brand — a Spanish renovation & maintenance services company. The stack is TypeScript/React with Prisma ORM (PostgreSQL). The architecture divides into public-facing pages (landing with multi-language i18n via data/*.ts files, contact form, chat/call widgets) and an Admin panel (analytics dashboard, mailing campaigns, user/auth management, AI settings, security ops). Key integrations include Vapi for voice calls, OpenAI for AI chat assistants, SMTP/mailing engine for email campaigns, and Google Analytics. Notable hotspots: the analytics overview route (309 fan-in) that queries visit/lead/chat data with remote production fallback; the chatActionAuth HMAC token system (complexity 23); and a 600+ line mailing service handling campaigns, templates, audiences, click/open tracking, SMTP delivery, and webhook processing. The admin panel offers role-based access (owner, admin, marketing). The project has high structural risk (5236 nodes, 27551 edges, 20 hotspots) and lacks a formal test suite — the only 'tests' are inline helper functions and synthetic delivery classifiers."
}
```

baseline.response:
Here is a concise technical orientation of the WORKMAT project.

```json
{
  "primary_language": "TypeScript",
  "entry_points": [
    "src/app/layout.tsx (Root layout with i18n, auth, theme providers)",
    "src/app/page.tsx (Homepage – default Spanish locale via HomeApp component)",
    "src/app/[slug]/page.tsx (Dynamic SEO section pages)",
    "src/app/admin/page.tsx (Admin panel – backups, deploy, users, analytics, env, integrations, maintenance)",
    "src/app/admin/mailing/page.tsx (Mailing campaign admin – large E2E email system)",
    "src/app/api/ (50+ API routes under src/app/api/ for auth, admin, backups, chat, vapi, deploy, maintenance, etc.)",
    "next.config.ts (Next.js config with webpack overrides, rewrites, redirects, headers)"
  ],
  "hotspots": [
    "src/app/admin/page.tsx (~4000 lines, main admin SPA with every operational section)",
    "src/app/admin/mailing/page.tsx (~3500 lines, full email campaign manager)",
    "prisma/schema.prisma (30+ models: User, Session, Call, ChatSession, MailCampaign, BackupRunEvent, Visitor, etc.)",
    "src/app/api/chat/route.ts (Chat system with message handling)",
    "src/app/api/vapi_chat/route.ts (Vapi.ai voice/call integration)",
    "src/app/api/admin/health/overview/route.ts (System health dashboard backend)",
    "src/app/api/admin/analytics/overview/route.ts (Analytics aggregation backend)",
    "src/app/api/deploy/ (Deploy pipeline: push, config, history, status, precheck, prisma-diff, logs/stream)"
  ],
  "relevant_tests": [
    "tests/admin-route-audit.test.ts (Verifies admin route guard detection logic)",
    "tests/language-runtime.test.ts (i18n runtime behavior)",
    "tests/home-navigation.test.ts (Homepage navigation flows)",
    "tests/admin-env-config.test.ts (Admin env configuration parsing)",
    "tests/mailing-formatters.test.ts (Mailing template formatting utilities)",
    "tests/hero-layout-math.test.ts (Hero section layout calculations)"
  ],
  "summary": "WORKMAT is a Next.js 15 (React 19) multi-language (es/en/pt/de/zh) SaaS web application with a PostgreSQL backend (Prisma ORM). It provides a VoIP/chat AI assistant system (Vapi.ai + Daily.co), automated PostgreSQL backups (pgBackRest), a full email marketing campaign manager (Nodemailer + tracking + mirror pages), SSH-based remote deployment pipelines, server maintenance automation, analytics tracking, user management with MFA/TOTP, and a comprehensive dark-themed admin panel. Styling uses Tailwind CSS v4, tests use Node:test + Playwright."
}
```
